IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: PROVISION OF INPATIENT AND OUTPATIENT DRUG & ALCOHOL REHABILITATION SERVICES, MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES, AND PRIMARY CARE SERVICES.
IRS990.MissionDesc: TARZANA TREATMENT CENTERS' (TTC) MISSION IS TO PROVIDE HIGH QUALITY, INTEGRATED HEALTH CARE THAT IMPROVES THE QUALITY OF LIFE AND HEALTH OF PATIENTS REGARDLESS OF FINANCIAL RESOURCES, AND CONTRIBUTES TO A REDUCTION IN THE TOTAL COST OF CARE. TTC IS A LEADER IN PROVIDING INTEGRATED HEALTH CARE SERVICES FOR SUBSTANCE USE DISORDERS, MENTAL ILLNESS AND PRIMARY MEDICAL CARE, INCLUDING SPECIALTY HIV/AIDS MEDICAL CARE.
IRS990.Desc: TTC'S EFFORTS TO IMPROVE SERVICE OUTCOMES ARE FOCUSED ON THREE MAJOR PROGRAM SERVICES:1) SUBSTANCE USE DISORDERS SUD TREATMENT SERVICES (FORMERLY CALLED ALCOHOL AND OTHER DRUG - AOD)TTC HAS BEEN A LEADER IN PROVIDING SUBSTANCE ABUSE TREATMENT SERVICES IN LOS ANGELES COUNTY. TTC TREATS ADDICTIONS TO ALL HABIT-FORMING SUBSTANCES. TTC PROVIDES DETOXIFICATION, RESIDENTIAL AND OUTPATIENT TREATMENT SERVICES TO ADULT AND YOUTH POPULATIONS WHICH ARE APPROPRIATE AND CULTURALLY RESPONSIVE. TTC'S COMMITMENT TO INTEGRATED BEHAVIORAL HEALTHCARE HELPS TO FOCUS ON RECOVERING FOR AN ADDICTION WITHOUT THE ADDITIONAL STRESS OF SEEKING OUTSIDE HELP FOR MEDICAL CARE OR MENTAL HEALTH TREATMENT. IN 2023, TTC SERVED A TOTAL OF 9,871 SUD PATIENTS: 1) 3,380 (34%) RECEIVED DETOXIFICATION SERVICES; 2) 2,845 (29%) PARTICIPATED IN RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT; 3) 3,646 (37%) RECEIVED OUTPATIENT SERVICES INCLUDING MAT SERVICES.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: 2) MENTAL HEALTH SERVICESSINCE 1972, TTC HAS BEEN PROVIDING SERVICES TO INDIVIDUALS AND FAMILIES WITH MENTAL HEALTH ISSUES, INCLUDING THOSE WITH CO-OCCURRING SUBSTANCE USE DISORDERS AND MENTAL HEALTH ISSUES. TTC AIMS THAT A MAJORITY OF PATIENTS SERVED WILL IMPROVE PHYSICAL AND MENTAL HEALTH CONDITIONS AND WILL BE DRUG-FREE. OVER THE PAST 15 YEARS, TTC HAS DEVELOPED AN EXPANDING AND COMPREHENSIVE ARRAY OF STAND-ALONE MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES. DURING 2023, A TOTAL OF 2,478 PATIENTS WITH SUBSTANCE ABUSE AND MENTAL HEALTH DISORDERS PARTICIPATED IN A CO-OCCURRING TREATMENT PROGRAM.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: 3) PRIMARY AND SPECIAL CARE SERVICESTTC IS LICENSED BY THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH SERVICES TO PROVIDE PRIMARY CARE SERVICES. TTC HAS FIVE PRIMARY MEDICAL CARE FACILITIES LOCATED IN THE SAN FERNANDO AND ANTELOPE VALLEYS AND ONE INLONG BEACH. TTC'S MISSION IS ADDRESSING THE HEALTH CARE NEEDS OF THE COMMUNITY'S UNDERSERVED POPULATION. DURING 2023, TTC'S PRIMARY CARE CLINICS SERVED A TOTAL OF 17,343 PATIENTS WITH AN AVERAGE OF 2.62 VISITS PER PATIENT. ALMOST ALL PATIENTS SERVED WERE BELOW 100% OF THE FEDERAL POVERTY LEVEL. A TOTAL OF 10,741 PATIENTS (29,372 VISITS) RECEIVED PRIMARY CARE IN THE SAN FERNANDO VALLEY, 3,125 PATIENTS (8,403 VISITS) OBTAINED SERVICES IN THE ANTELOPE VALLEY, 2,368 (5,874 VISITS) HAD SERVICES IN PALMDALE AND 1,109 (1,851 VISITS) HAD SERVICES IN LONG BEACH DURING 2023. ON AVERAGE, PATIENTS HAD 2.73 VISITS PER PATIENT IN THE SAN FERNANDO VALLEY, 2.69 VISITS PER PATIENT IN ANTELOPE VALLEY, 2.48 VISITS PER PATIENT IN PALMDALE AND 1.67 VISITS PER PATIENT IN LONG BEACH.TTC AND IT'S HIV COMMUNITY CLINIC ARE SUCCEEDING IN ASSISTING THE GOVERNMENT IN MEETING THE NATIONAL HEALTHY PEOPLE 2013 OBJECTIVES FOR EXPANDING HIV COUNSELING AND TESTING SERVICES AND INCREASING THE NUMBER OF PRIMARY CARE PROGRAMS THAT PROVIDE COMPREHENSIVE HIV SERVICES. TTC'S EFFORTS ARE CONTANTLY GEARED TOWARDS MEETING THE DEMAND OF UNDERSERVED PERSONS LIVING WITH HIV/AIDS (PLWHA) IN L.A. COUNTY SEEKING PRIMARY CARE SERVICES.

ProgSrvcAccomActyOtherGrp.Desc: OTHER SERVICES PROVIDED BY TTC ARE (1) RECOVERY BRIDGE HOUSING, (2) CASE MANAGEMENT AND RECOVERY SUPPORT SERVICES, (3) CLIENT ENGAGEMENT AND NAVIGATION SERVICES (CENS), (4) HIV AND HEP C SCREENING, (5) SMOKING CESSATION/TOBACCO CONTROL, (6) METHADONE MAINTENANCE, (7) VIVITROL, (8) MEDICATION ASSISTED TREATMENT-MAT, (9) WRAPAROUND PROGRAM, (10) PBHCI, (11) TELEHEALTH, (12) HEALTH HOME PROGRAM AND (14) YOUTH SERVICES. ALL STATISTICAL INFORMATION PROVIDED WAS BASED ON ACTUAL DATA GENERATED FROM TTC'S DATABASE SYSTEM.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: TTC COLLEGE WAS AWARDED A CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE ACCESS AND INFORMATION (HCAI) PEER PERSONNEL TRAINING AND PLACEMENT PROGRAM.

SupplementalInformationGrp.ExplanationTxt: PART V, SECTION B, LINE 5: THE PROCESS TO IDENTIFY AND PRIORITIZE THE HEALTH NEEDS OF THE COMMUNITY INVOLVED DESIGNING THE ASSESSMENT TOOLS, COLLECTING PRIMARY AND SECONDARY DATA, AND ANALYZING THE DATA. THE TEAM IDENTIFIED EXISTING STUDIES IN THE COMMUNITY THROUGH REVIEW OF EPIDEMIOLOGICAL DATA, SURVEYS OF COMMUNITY MEMBERS AND CONSUMERS OF MENTAL HEALTH AND ADDICTIONS SERVICES, AND KEY INFORMANT INTERVIEWS WITH DIRECT SERVICE PROVIDERS IN THE COMMUNITY.

PART V, SECTION B, LINE 11: BASED ON CONSOLIDATION OF HEALTH NEEDS IDENTIFIED BY THE PATIENT SURVEY AND KEY INFORMANTS, TTC WILL FOCUS ON (1) CONTINUE TO PROVIDE THE FULL CONTINUUM OF SUD TREATMENT SERVICES IN THE COMMUNITY WITH AN EMPHASIS ON PROVIDING TARGETED OUTREACH AND ENGAGEMENT ACTIVITIES TO HOMELESS INDIVIDUALS, WHICH WERE IDENTIFIED IN THE KEY INFORMANT INTERVIEWS AS THE MOST VULNERABLE POPULATION (2) CONTINUE TO PROVIDE THE FULL CONTINUUM OF MH TREATMENT SERVICES TO ADDRESS STIGMA AND SERIOUS MENTAL ILLNESS (SMI) IN THE COMMUNITY WITH AN EMPHASIS ON INCREASING COMMUNITY KNOWLEDGE AND ACCESS TO UNDERUTILIZED PROGRAMS FOR CHILDREN AND YOUTH SUCH AS EPSDT, PEI AND MILD TO MODERATE SERVICES. (3) CONTINUE FOCUS ON INTEGRATING BEHAVIORAL HEALTH AND MEDICAL CARE SERVICES BY FOCUSING ON CHRONIC DISEASES PREVALENT IN THE COMMUNITIES WE SERVE (E.G. DIABETES, OBESITY, ASTHMA, HIGH BLOOD PRESSURE, ETC.) AND IT'S INTERACTION WITH SUD/MH. THE HEALTH NEEDS INCLUDING AFFORDABLE HOUSING/HOUSING INSTABILITY, LIMITED AND UNRELIABLE TRANSPORTATION OPTIONS, AND FOOD INSECURITY WILL NOT BE ADDRESSED DIRECTLY DUE TO THE LACK OF FINANCIAL AND STAFF RESOURCES TO PROVIDE SERVICES TO ADDRESS SUCH NEEDS. HOWEVER, TARZANA TREATMENT CENTERS WILL CONTINUE TO COLLABORATE WITH COMMUNITY PARTNER AGENCIES BY PROVIDING REFERRALS TO HELP PATIENTS MEET THEIR OWN HEALTH NEEDS.

OthHlthCareFcltsGrp.FacilityTxt: PRIMARY MEDICAL CARE, SUBSTANCE ABUSE & MENTAL HEALTH, ETC.
RESIDENTIAL ALCOHOL & DRUG TREATMENT, MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES, ETC.
TEEN & YOUNG ADULT SPECIFIC SERVICES, ETC.

TEEN & YOUNG ADULT SPECIFIC SERVICES, ETC.
FAMILY MEDICAL CARE, PRIMARY CARE, ETC.
FAMILY MEDICAL CARE, PRIMARY CARE, ETC.
MENTAL HEALTH, ALCOHOL & DRUG TREATMENT, ETC.
MENTAL HEALTH, ALCOHOL & DRUG TREATMENT, ETC.
MENTAL HEALTH, ALC & DRUG TRTMNT, ECM
SUBSTANCE USE, MENTAL HEALTH, SOBER CNTR, ECM

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: DIRECT AND ALLOCATED INDIRECT PROGRAM COSTS REPRESENTS THE TOTAL PROGRAM COSTS. TOTAL PROGRAM COSTS IN EXCESS OF PROGRAM REVENUE (FROM ALL SOURCES) ARE DEEMED TO BE THE CHARITY AT COST.

TTC'S FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE POLICIES CLEARLY STATE THE ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA (I.E. INCOME, ASSETS) AND THE PROCESS USED BY TTC TO DETERMINE WHETHER A PATIENT IS ELIGIBLE FOR FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE. SUCH PROCESS TAKES INTO ACCOUNT WHERE AND HOW FAR A PARTICULAR PATIENT FALLS RELATIVE TO EXISTING FEDERAL POVERTY LEVELS (FPL).ANY PATIENT WHO BELIEVES THAT THEY ARE QUALIFIED MAY APPLY FOR FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE UNDER TTC'S CHARITY CARE POLICY, DISCOUNT PAYMENT AND/OR SLIDING FEE POLICY. TTC STAFF USE THEIR BEST EFFORTS TO ENSURE ALL FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE POLICIES ARE APPLIED CONSISTENTLY AND USE A SLIDING FEE SCALE AS REQUIRED BY TTC'S FQHC LOOK-ALIKE STATUS. IN DETERMINING A PATIENT'S ELIGIBILITY FOR FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE, TTC STAFF ASSISTS THE PATIENT IN DETERMINING IF HE/SHE IS ELIGIBLE FOR GOVERNMENT-SPONSORED PROGRAMS.

DIRECTORS ENSURE THAT APPROPRIATE STAFF MEMBERS ARE KNOWLEDGEABLE ABOUT THE EXISTENCE OF THE FACILITY'S FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE POLICIES. WHEN COMMUNICATING TO PATIENTS REGARDING THE FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE/SLIDING FEE POLICY, STAFF DO SO IN THE PRIMARY LANGUAGE OF THE PATIENT, OR HIS/HER FAMILY, IF REASONABLY POSSIBLE, AND IN A MANNER CONSISTENT WITH ALL APPLICABLE FEDERAL AND STATE LAWS AND REGULATIONS. TTC SHARES THE FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE/SLIDING FEE POLICY WITH APPROPRIATE COMMUNITY HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES AGENCIES AND OTHER ORGANIZATIONS THAT ASSIST SUCH PATIENTS. EACH FACILITY POSTS NOTICES REGARDING THE AVAILABILITY OF FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE/SLIDING FEE TO LOW-INCOME UNINSURED PATIENTS. THESE NOTICES ARE POSTED IN VISIBLE LOCATIONS SUCH AS ADMITTING/REGISTRATION AND BILLING OFFICE. EVERY POSTED NOTICE REGARDING FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE/SLIDING FEE POLICIES CONTAINS BRIEF INSTRUCTIONS ON HOW TO APPLY FOR CHARITY CARE OR A DISCOUNTED PAYMENT.

TTC IS LOCATED IN THE WESTERN HALF OF THE SAN FERNANDO VALLEY (SFV) WITHIN LOS ANGELES COUNTY. THIS AREA OF APPROXIMATELY 200 SQUARE MILES INCLUDES THE DENSELY POPULATED CENTRAL PORTIONS OF THE SFV, WHICH CONSISTS PRIMARILY OF SUBURBAN AND COMMERCIAL DEVELOPMENT. THE FAR WESTERN PORTIONS OF THE SFV ARE MORE SPARSELY POPULATED, BUT HAVE SHOWN INCREASED SUBURBAN AND COMMERICAL DEVELOPMENT IN RECENT YEARS. THIS FAR WESTERN PORTION HAS THE FASTEST GROWING POPULATION OF THE REGION. TTC TREATS INDIVIDUALS FROM THE ENTIRE LOS ANGELES COUNTY. APPROXIMATELY 1.8 MILLION IN THE SAN FERNANDO VALLEY.IT IS UNDERSTANDABLE THAT WE WOULD SEE A LARGER PROPORTION OF CLIENTS (ESTIMATED AT 537,973) FROM OUR MORE IMMEDIATE GEOGRAPHIC AREA. THE CITIES THAT COMPRISE OUR IMMEDIATE SERVICE AREA INCLUDE AGOURA, CALABASAS, CANOGA PARK, CHATSWORTH, ENCINO, HIDDEN HILLS, NORTHRIDGE, PORTER RANCH, RESEDA, SHERMAN OAKS, TARZANA, TOPANGA, WINNETKA AND WOODLAND HILLS. THE ZIP CODES INCLUDED IN OUR IMMEDIATE SERVICE AREA ARE 90290, 91301 THROUGH 91306, 91311, 91316, 91324, 91335, 91364, 91367, 91406, 91423 AND 91436.BASED ON THE AMERICAN COMMUNITY SURVEY IN 2023, HISPANIC ORIGIN CONSTITUTE ABOUT 42% OF THE SAN FERNANDO VALLEY HOUSEHOLD POPULATION, WHICH IS SOMEWHAT LESS THAN THE 47% HISPANIC IN LOS ANGELES CITY AND THE 49% IN THE LOS ANGELES COUNTY. NON-HISPANIC WHITES COMPRISE 39% OF THE HOUSEHOLD POPULATION, WHICH IS SLIGHTLY LOWER THAN THE HISPANIC PERCENTAGE. THE ASIAN POPULATION IS 11% AND AFRICAN-AMERICANS CONSTITUTE ABOUT 4%.HIGH SCHOOL DIPLOMAS HAVE BEEN EARNED BY 83% OF THE 25 YEARS AND OLDER POPULATION, AND THE PROPORTION EXCEEDS 90% IN SEVERAL COMMUNITIES. 38% OF THE POPULATION HAVE EARNED A BACHELOR DEGREE OR HIGHER. 66.4% OF THE POPULATION (16 AND OVER) PARTICIPATES IN THE LABOR FORCE. THE PROPORTION IS FAIRLY CONSTANT ACROSS THE CITIES OF SAN FERNANDO VALLEY. THE EMPLOYED RESIDENTS TOTALED 992,371 IN 2023. THE SFV MEDIAN HOUSEHOLD INCOME EXCEEDED $90,176 IN 2023, TOPPING LOS ANGELES CITY'S BY $10,475, AND LOS ANGELES COUNTY'S BY $3,589, BUT FALLING ABOUT $5,345 BELOW CALIFORNIA'S MEDIAN. THE RACIAL COMPOSITION OF THE SFV IS 39% NON-HISPANIC WHITE, 41.5% LATINO, 11.3% ASIAN/PACIFIC ISLANDER, AND 4.0% AFRICAN-AMERICAN. WITH RESPECT TO EDUCATION, WITHIN THE SFV, APPROXIMATELY 16.9% OF THE POPULATION, 25 YEARS AND OLDER, HAVE NOT COMPLETED HIGH SCHOOL EDUCATION. INCOME LEVELS ILLUSTRATE THE DISPARITIES THAT EXIST IN SPA 2. APPROXIMATELY 15% OF THE HOUSEHOLDS IN THE SFV REPORTED ANNUAL INCOMES BELOW $25,000. THE RACIAL AND ETHNIC COMPOSITION OF PATIENTS TREATED IN TARZANA'S INPATIENT UNIT IN 2023-2024 IS 36% NON-HISPANIC WHITE, 50% LATINO, 11.5% AFRICAN-AMERICAN AND 1.2% ASIAN-PACIFIC ISLANDER. ONLY 9.7% WERE WORKING FULL-TIME/PART-TIME, 2.1% WERE NOT IN THE LABOR FORCE AND 60.5% UNEMPLOYED SEEKING WORK. ABOUT 78% ARE MALE, 83% HAVE COMPLETED 12TH GRADE, AND 25% HAVE COMPLETED AT LEAST TWO YEARS OF COLLEGE. THE MOST FREQUENT REASON FOR ADMISSION WAS DETOXIFICATION AND STABILIZATION FROM USING ALCOHOL (63.7%) AND HEROIN (24.8%).
THE COMPENSATION IS BASED ON COMPARABLE DATA AND ANALYSIS. THE DIRECTORS' DISCUSSION AND DECISION IS CONTEMPORANEOUSLY INCLUDED IN THE BOARD MINUTES.

THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS APPROVED THE FUNDING OF DEFERRED COMPENSATION FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDED JUNE 30, 2024 IN THE AMOUNT OF $ 669,497. THE AMOUNTS INCLUDED AS OTHER COMPENSATION ARE THE ACTUAL PAYMENTS MADE FROM THE DEFERRED COMPENSATION PLAN AS APPROVED BY THE INDEPENDENT BOARD OF DIRECTORS IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE PLAN. THIS COMPENSATION REPRESENTS SERVICE IN EXCESS OF 40 YEARS FOR EACH BENEFICIARY OF THE PLAN.

BusTrInvolveInterestedPrsnGrp.TransactionDesc: LEASETTC RENTED PROPERTY FROM RELATED PERSON AT FAIR MARKET VALUE AS DETERMINED BY INDEPENDENT APPRAISER AND APPROVED BY INDEPENDENT MEMBERS OF THE TTC BOARD OF DIRECTORS. BOARD MEMBERS FRACTIONAL SHARE (RELATED PORTION) OF RENT AND PROPERTY TAXES PAID.

LEASETTC RENTED PROPERTY FROM RELATED PERSON AT FAIR MARKET VALUE AND APPROVED BY INDEPENDENT MEMBERS OF THE TTC BOARD OF DIRECTORS. BOARD MEMBERS FRACTIONAL SHARE (RELATED PORTION) OF RENT AND PROPERTY TAXES PAID.

LEASETTC RENTED PROPERTY FROM RELATED PERSON AT FAIR MARKET VALUE AS DETERMINED BY INDEPENDENT APPRAISER AND APPROVED BY INDEPENDENT MEMBERS OF THE TTC BOARD OF DIRECTORS. BOARD MEMBERS FRACTIONAL SHARE (RELATED PORTION) OF RENT AND PROPERTY TAXES PAID.

LEASETTC RENTED PROPERTY FROM RELATED PERSON AT FAIR MARKET VALUE AS DETERMINED BY INDEPENDENT APPRAISER AND APPROVED BY INDEPENDENT MEMBERS OF THE TTC BOARD OF DIRECTORS. BOARD MEMBERS FRACTIONAL SHARE (RELATED PORTION) OF RENT AND PROPERTY TAXES PAID.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 2
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ANDREW KIRSHBERG AND NORA MARTELLA KIRSHBERG ARE HUSBAND AND WIFE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE INITIAL DRAFT FORM 990 IS PREPARED BY TTC'S TAX ACCOUNTANT. THE DRAFT FORM 990 IS REVIEWED BY THE PRESIDENT/CHIEF OPERATING OFFICER AND THEN BY OUTSIDE COUNSEL. AFTER MAKING ANY NECESSARY MODIFICATIONS, THE REVISED DRAFT FORM 990 IS PROVIDED TO THE AUDIT COMMITTEE OF TTC'S BOARD OF DIRECTORS, THEN TO THE FULL BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF TTC FOR APPROVAL. SUBJECT TO SUCH APPROVAL AND ANY REQUESTED MODIFICATIONS, THE FORM 990 IS FINALIZED FOR SIGNATURE AND FILING.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: AT LEAST ANNUALLY, TTC DISTRIBUTES A CERTIFICATION AND DISCLOSURE FORM TO ALL BOARD MEMBERS AND KEY EMPLOYEES, ATTACHING THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY. THOSE PERSONS ARE REQUIRED TO COMPLETE AND SIGN THE CERTIFICATION AND DISCLOSURE FORM, INDICATING THEY HAVE RECEIVED AND UNDERSTAND THE POLICY, AND ARE IN COMPLIANCE, OR DISCLOSE POSSIBLE CONFLICTS WITH THE POLICY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ON A BI-ANNUAL BASIS, TARZANA TREATMENT CENTERS, INC. CONSULTS WITH INDEPENDENT HUMAN RESOURCE EXPERTS REGARDING THE BENEFITS AND COMPENSATION PROVIDED IN IT'S EMPLOYMENT PACKAGE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 18
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: TTC WILL PROVIDE FORM 990 UPON REQUEST.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: TTC WILL PROVIDE ITS GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.

IdRelatedTaxExemptOrgGrp.PrimaryActivitiesTxt: TO HOLD ASSETS AND INVEST IN FURTHERANCE OF TTC'S CHARITABLE PURPOSE
